title: loop.plan
description: The loop.plan artifact: goal, intent, success/failure criteria, termination bounds, constraints, and a DAG of nodes. Field names and enum values are locked byte-for-byte per references/loop_plan_spec.md. Edges are modelled implicitly via each node's requires list (produces/requires artifact dependencies); there is no separate edge object.

schema_version.description: Version of the loop.plan schema this document conforms to (e.g. "1.0.0"). Distinct from plan_version.

plan_id.description: Stable unique identifier for this plan. Primary key across the checkpoint, node contracts, and evidence ledger.

true_intent.description: The underlying intent surfaced by the interview — what success really means, which may be broader or narrower than goal.

non_goals.description: Explicitly out-of-scope outcomes. May be empty.

failure_criteria.description: Conditions that mean the task has failed and must stop or escalate. May be empty.

termination.description: Bounds and the done condition for the whole loop.

constraints.description: Hard constraints the plan and its execution must respect (budget, tech, policy). May be empty.

nodes.description: The DAG. Each entry is a node object. Must be non-empty.

created.description: Date the plan was created (ISO-8601 date or date-time).

plan_version.description: Monotonic integer, incremented each time the plan is re-generated (replan). The plan for a given plan_version is immutable.

status.description: Node status. The canonical, complete set of 15 node statuses. Terminal: completed, cancelled, deprecated.

id.description: Unique id of the criterion within the plan.

measurable.description: true if the criterion has an objective, checkable test; false if it is a judgement call (which should be gated by llm_judge or human_approval).

max_wall_clock_hours.description: Wall-clock budget in hours, or null for unbounded.

max_cost_units.description: Budget in abstract cost units, or null for unbounded. Tracked against checkpoint cost_units_spent.

done_when.description: The single overriding done condition, typically "all success_criteria met and all top-level nodes completed".

gate.description: The evidence gate. A node cannot transition to completed until its gate passes.

threshold.description: Pass threshold for scored gates (0..1). null for pass/fail gates.

rubric.description: Path to a rubric document for judged gates, or null.

evidence_ref.description: Path where this gate's evidence is written.

max_attempts.description: Maximum number of execution attempts for this node before escalating past local_retry.

backoff_base_seconds.description: Base backoff between attempts (multiplied per attempt for exponential backoff).

jitter.description: true to add randomised jitter to backoff.

subgraph.description: A nested loop.plan fragment (isomorphic), materialised by a mapper node or any node with allow_subgraph: true.

parent_ref.description: The id of the parent node in the enclosing plan that owns this subgraph.

schema_version.description: Same meaning as top-level; the fragment conforms to the same schema.

plan_version.description: Version of this fragment; incremented on replan of the fragment.

nodes.description: The fragment's nodes. Typically design_invariant: false (runtime-discovered).

node.description: A node in the DAG. Edges are modelled via the requires list (produces/requires artifact dependencies).

id.description: Unique node identifier within the plan (and within any subgraph fragment). Referenced by other nodes' requires.

kind.description: One of the 8 node kinds. Default is milestone.

design_invariant.description: true if this node is design-time-invariant (belongs at the top level); false if runtime-discovered (belongs inside a subgraph).

status.description: Current status. One of the 15 node statuses.

requires.description: Ids of nodes this node depends on. This node is ready only when every id here is completed. May be empty for a root node.

produces.description: Artifact paths or ids this node produces. May be empty.

inputs.description: Inputs consumed by this node (artifact refs, parameters). Feeds the node's cache_key. May be empty.

preconditions.description: Conditions that must hold before the node may run, beyond requires. May be empty.

postconditions.description: Conditions guaranteed true after successful completion. May be empty.

gate.description: The evidence gate. null only for trivial nodes.

on_failure.description: Where this node starts on the escalation ladder.

priority.description: Scheduling priority among simultaneously-ready nodes (higher runs first).

parallelizable.description: true if this node may run concurrently with other ready nodes.

allow_subgraph.description: true if this node may materialise a subgraph at runtime when it discovers it is too big/dark/complex.

subgraph.description: A nested loop.plan fragment, or null if none has been materialised.

notes.description: Free-form notes. May be empty string.
